coredata.dc:title: Combined First-principles and Experimental Studies of a V-doped LiFePO4/C Composite as a Cathode Material for Lithium-ion Batteries
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2021 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Zhao, Qunfang
Zhang, Shuqiong
Wang, Chang
Jiang, Guanghui
Hu, Minyi
Chen, Zhaoyong
Ouyang, Quansheng
coredata.dc:description: As a promising cathode material for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), olivine-structured LiFePO4 has been increasingly studied due to its high capacity, excellent thermal stability, cycling stability and environmental friendliness. Nevertheless, the main challenge of LiFePO4 is its poor conductivity and Li+ diffusion coefficient. Herein, a V-doped LiFePO4/C composite is prepared via a solid-state method. The electronic structures of the samples are obtained by first-principles calculations based on density functional theory. V2 exhibits enhanced electrochemical performance, demonstrating discharge capacities as high as 155.6, 150.2, 136.0, 122.4 and 93.1 mA h g-1 at 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0 and 3.0C, respectively, and approximately 100% capacity retention after 100 cycles at 1.0C.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Lithium-ion battery
LiFePO4/C
First-principles
V-doped

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref
xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2021 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Combined First-principles and Experimental Studies of a V-doped LiFePO4 /C Composite as a Cathode Material for Lithium-ion BatteriesQunfang Zhao, 1 2 3 Shuqiong Zhang, 1 2 3 Chang Wang, 1 2 3 Guanghui Jiang, 1 2 3 Minyi Hu, 1 2 3 Zhaoyong Chen, 4 Quansheng Ouyang, 1 2 3 qsouyang@163.com 1 Advanced Batteries and Materials Engineering Research Center, Guizhou Light Industry Technical College, Guiyang 550025, China; Advanced Batteries and Materials Engineering Research Center Guizhou Light Industry Technical College Guiyang 550025 China Advanced Batteries and Materials Engineering Research Center, Guizhou Light Industry Technical College, Guiyang 550025, China; 2 Graphene Materials Engineering Research Center of Guizhou Colleges and Universities, Guiyang 550025, China; Graphene Materials Engineering Research Center of Guizhou Colleges and Universities Guiyang 550025 China Graphene Materials Engineering Research Center of Guizhou Colleges and Universities, Guiyang 550025, China; 3 Provincial Collaborative Innovation Center of Used Power Batteries Recycling, Guiyang 550025, China; Provincial Collaborative Innovation Center of Used Power Batteries Recycling Guiyang 550025 China Provincial Collaborative Innovation Center of Used Power Batteries Recycling, Guiyang 550025, China; 4 School of Materials Science and Engineering, Changsha University of Science and Technology, Changsha 410114, China; School of Materials Science and Engineering Changsha University of Science and Technology Changsha 410114 China School of Materials Science and Engineering, Changsha University of Science and Technology, Changsha 410114, China; As a promising cathode material for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), olivine-structured LiFePO4 has been increasingly studied due to its high capacity, excellent thermal stability, cycling stability and environmental friendliness. Nevertheless, the main challenge of LiFePO has been increasingly studied due to its high capacity, excellent thermal stability, cycling stability and environmental friendliness. Nevertheless, the main challenge of LiFePO4 is its poor conductivity and Li is its poor conductivity and Li+ diffusion coefficient. Herein, a V-doped LiFePO diffusion coefficient. Herein, a V-doped LiFePO4 /C composite is prepared via a solid-state method. The electronic structures of the samples are obtained by first-principles calculations based on density functional theory. V2 exhibits enhanced electrochemical performance, demonstrating discharge capacities as high as 155.6, 150.2, 136.0, 122.4 and 93.1 mA h g /C composite is prepared via a solid-state method. The electronic structures of the samples are obtained by first-principles calculations based on density functional theory. V2 exhibits enhanced electrochemical performance, demonstrating discharge capacities as high as 155.6, 150.2, 136.0, 122.4 and 93.1 mA h g-1 at 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0 and 3.0C, respectively, and approximately 100% capacity retention after 100 cycles at 1.0C.Keywords Lithium-ion battery LiFePO4 /CFirst-principles V-doped References 1. head.ce:title: Combined First-principles and Experimental Studies of a V-doped LiFePO4/C Composite as a Cathode Material for Lithium-ion Batteries

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Advanced Batteries and Materials Engineering Research Center, Guizhou Light Industry Technical College, Guiyang 550025, China;

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Advanced Batteries and Materials Engineering Research Center

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Advanced Batteries and Materials Engineering Research Center, Guizhou Light Industry Technical College, Guiyang 550025, China;

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Graphene Materials Engineering Research Center of Guizhou Colleges and Universities, Guiyang 550025, China;

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Graphene Materials Engineering Research Center of Guizhou Colleges and Universities

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Graphene Materials Engineering Research Center of Guizhou Colleges and Universities, Guiyang 550025, China;

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Provincial Collaborative Innovation Center of Used Power Batteries Recycling, Guiyang 550025, China;

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Provincial Collaborative Innovation Center of Used Power Batteries Recycling

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Provincial Collaborative Innovation Center of Used Power Batteries Recycling, Guiyang 550025, China;

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Materials Science and Engineering, Changsha University of Science and Technology, Changsha 410114, China;

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Materials Science and Engineering
Changsha University of Science and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Materials Science and Engineering, Changsha University of Science and Technology, Changsha 410114, China;

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: As a promising cathode material for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), olivine-structured LiFePO4 has been increasingly studied due to its high capacity, excellent thermal stability, cycling stability and environmental friendliness. Nevertheless, the main challenge of LiFePO4 is its poor conductivity and Li+ diffusion coefficient. Herein, a V-doped LiFePO4/C composite is prepared via a solid-state method. The electronic structures of the samples are obtained by first-principles calculations based on density functional theory. V2 exhibits enhanced electrochemical performance, demonstrating discharge capacities as high as 155.6, 150.2, 136.0, 122.4 and 93.1 mA h g-1 at 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0 and 3.0C, respectively, and approximately 100% capacity retention after 100 cycles at 1.0C.

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: X. Kong, Y. Zhang, S. Peng, J. Zeng, J. Zhang, ACS Sustainable Chem., 8 (2020) 1493.
